fr.summary: Un cadre d'apprentissage automatique utilise des capteurs de pieds portables surveillant les modèles de température et de pression pour détecter le risque précoce d'ulcère diabétique du pied, les algorithmes non supervisés identifiant efficacement les micro-anomalies pour une intervention préventive.

de.title: Prädiktive Analytik für Fußgeschwüre unter Verwendung von Zeitreihen-Temperatur- und Druckdaten
de.summary: Ein Machine-Learning-Framework nutzt tragbare Fußsensoren, die Temperatur- und Druckmuster überwachen, um das Frührisiko eines diabetischen Fußgeschwürs zu erkennen. Unüberwachte Algorithmen identifizieren Mikro-Anomalien wirksam für präventive Maßnahmen.

es.title: Análisis predictivo de úlceras en los pies utilizando datos de temperatura y presión en series de tiempo
es.summary: Un marco de aprendizaje automático utiliza sensores de pie portátiles que monitorean patrones de temperatura y presión para detectar el riesgo temprano de úlcera diabética en el pie, con algoritmos no supervisados identificando efectivamente micro-anomalías para intervención preventiva.

title: The DIME Architecture: A Unified Operational Algorithm for Neural Representation, Dynamics, Control and Integration
summary: DIME proposes a unified neural architecture integrating perception, memory, valuation, and conscious access through engrams and marker systems, providing a computational framework consistent with empirical neuroscience evidence.
content:
arXiv:2603.12286v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Modern neuroscience has accumulated extensive evidence on perception, memory, prediction, valuation, and consciousness, yet still lacks an explicit operational architecture capable of integrating these phenomena within a unified computational framework. Existing theories address specific aspects of neural function: predictive coding and active inference emphasize hierarchical inference and prediction error minimization; engram theories explain memory through distributed cell assemblies; neuromodulatory accounts focus on value-dependent regulation of plasticity and behaviour; and global workspace or large-scale network models investigate mechanisms underlying conscious access. Despite their explanatory power, these approaches remain only partially integrated at the architectural level. This work introduces DIME (Detect-Integrate-Mark-Execute), a neural architecture organizing perception, memory, valuation, and conscious access within a common operational cycle. The framework includes four interacting components: engrams, distributed recurrent neural structures supporting multiple activation trajectories; execution threads, spatiotemporal trajectories implementing neural processes; marker systems, neuromodulatory and limbic mechanisms regulating gain, plasticity, and trajectory selection; and hyperengrams, large-scale integrative states associated with operational conscious access. The framework is consistent with empirical evidence from hippocampal indexing, recurrent cortical processing, replay phenomena, large-scale network integration, and neuromodulatory regulation. Formulated at an abstract computational level, DIME may also inform artificial intelligence and robotics by providing an architectural template in which representation, valuation, and temporal sequencing emerge from a unified mechanism. An extended theoretical exposition is available in a companion monograph on Zenodo.

zh.title: DIME架构:神经表征、动力学、控制和集成的统一运算算法
zh.summary: DIME提出了一个统一的神经架构,通过记忆印迹和标记系统整合感知、记忆、评价和意识访问,提供与实证神经科学证据一致的计算框架。

fr.title: L'architecture DIME : un algorithme opérationnel unifié pour la représentation neuronale, la dynamique, le contrôle et l'intégration
fr.summary: DIME propose une architecture neuronale unifiée intégrant la perception, la mémoire, l'évaluation et l'accès conscient par des systèmes d'engrams et de marqueurs, fournissant un cadre computationnel cohérent avec les preuves empiriques des neurosciences.

de.title: Die DIME-Architektur: ein einheitlicher Betriebsalgorithmus für neuronale Repräsentation, Dynamik, Kontrolle und Integration
de.summary: DIME schlägt eine einheitliche neuronale Architektur vor, die Wahrnehmung, Gedächtnis, Bewertung und bewussten Zugriff durch Engram- und Markersysteme integriert und einen Rechenrahmen bereitstellt, der mit empirischen Neurowissenschaftsnachweisen konsistent ist.

es.title: La arquitectura DIME: un algoritmo operacional unificado para representación neural, dinámica, control e integración
es.summary: DIME propone una arquitectura neural unificada que integra percepción, memoria, valuación y acceso consciente a través de sistemas de engramas y marcadores, proporcionando un marco computacional consistente con la evidencia empírica de neurociencia.

title: From Garbage to Gold: A Data-Architectural Theory of Predictive Robustness
summary: Researchers synthesize information theory and latent factor models to explain why high-dimensional, error-prone data enables robust predictions, proposing data-centric AI methods to identify efficient predictor sets overcoming conventional data-quality constraints.
content:
arXiv:2603.12288v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Tabular machine learning presents a paradox: modern models achieve state-of-the-art performance using high-dimensional (high-D), collinear, error-prone data, defying the "Garbage In, Garbage Out" mantra. To help resolve this, we synthesize principles from Information Theory, Latent Factor Models, and Psychometrics, clarifying that predictive robustness arises not solely from data cleanliness, but from the synergy between data architecture and model capacity. Partitioning predictor-space "noise" into "Predictor Error" and "Structural Uncertainty" (informational deficits from stochastic generative mappings), we prove that leveraging high-D sets of error-prone predictors asymptotically overcomes both types of noise, whereas cleaning a low-D set is fundamentally bounded by Structural Uncertainty. We demonstrate why "Informative Collinearity" (dependencies from shared latent causes) enhances reliability and convergence efficiency, and explain why increased dimensionality reduces the latent inference burden, enabling feasibility with finite samples. To address practical constraints, we propose "Proactive Data-Centric AI" to identify predictors that enable robustness efficiently. We also derive boundaries for Systematic Error Regimes and show why models that absorb "rogue" dependencies can mitigate assumption violations. Linking latent architecture to Benign Overfitting, we offer a first step towards a unified view of robustness to Outcome Error and predictor-space noise, while also delineating when traditional DCAI's focus on label cleaning remains powerful. By redefining data quality from item-level perfection to portfolio-level architecture, we provide a theoretical rationale for "Local Factories" -- learning from live, uncurated enterprise "data swamps" -- supporting a deployment paradigm shift from "Model Transfer" to "Methodology Transfer'' to overcome static generalizability limitations.

zh.title: 从垃圾到黄金：预测鲁棒性的数据架构理论
zh.summary: 研究人员综合信息论和潜因子模型，解释高维、容易出错的数据如何能够实现鲁棒预测，提出数据为中心的人工智能方法，以识别克服传统数据质量约束的高效预测因子集。

fr.title: De la Poubelle à l'Or : Une Théorie Architecturale des Données pour la Robustesse Prédictive
fr.summary: Les chercheurs synthétisent la théorie de l'information et les modèles de facteurs latents pour expliquer comment les données haute-dimensionnelles et sujettes aux erreurs permettent des prédictions robustes, en proposant des méthodes d'IA centrées sur les données pour identifier des ensembles de prédicteurs efficaces dépassant les contraintes conventionnelles de qualité des données.

de.title: Vom Müll zu Gold: Eine Daten-Architektur-Theorie der Vorhersagerobusten
de.summary: Forscher synthetisieren Informationstheorie und latente Faktormodelle, um zu erklären, warum hochdimensionale, fehleranfällige Daten robuste Vorhersagen ermöglichen, und schlagen datenorientierte KI-Methoden vor, um effiziente Prediktor-Sets zu identifizieren, die konventionelle Datenkwalitätsbeschränkungen überwinden.

es.title: De la Basura al Oro: Una Teoría de Arquitectura de Datos de Robustez Predictiva
es.summary: Los investigadores sintetizan la teoría de la información y modelos de factores latentes para explicar por qué los datos de alta dimensión propensos a errores permiten predicciones robustas, proponiendo métodos de IA centrados en datos para identificar conjuntos de predictores eficientes que superan las limitaciones convencionales de calidad de datos.

title: Detecting Miscitation on the Scholarly Web through LLM-Augmented Text-Rich Graph Learning
summary: LAGMiD detects scholarly miscitations by combining LLM semantic reasoning with graph neural networks through chain-of-thought prompting and knowledge distillation, reducing computational cost while identifying references that contradict their cited claims.
content:
arXiv:2603.12290v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Scholarly web is a vast network of knowledge connected by citations. However, this system is increasingly compromised by miscitation, where references do not support or even contradict the claims they are cited for. Current miscitation detection methods, which primarily rely on semantic similarity or network anomalies, struggle to capture the nuanced relationship between a citation's context and its place in the wider network. While large language models (LLMs) offer powerful capabilities in semantic reasoning for this task, their deployment is hindered by hallucination risks and high computational costs. In this work, we introduce LLM-Augmented Graph Learning-based Miscitation Detector (LAGMiD), a novel framework that leverages LLMs for deep semantic reasoning over citation graphs and distills this knowledge into graph neural networks (GNNs) for efficient and scalable miscitation detection. Specifically, LAGMiD introduces an evidence-chain reasoning mechanism, which uses chain-of-thought prompting, to perform multi-hop citation tracing and assess semantic fidelity. To reduce LLM inference costs, we design a knowledge distillation method aligning GNN embeddings with intermediate LLM reasoning states. A collaborative learning strategy further routes complex cases to the LLM while optimizing the GNN for structure-based generalization. Experiments on three real-world benchmarks show that LAGMiD achieves state-of-the-art miscitation detection with significantly reduced inference cost.

zh.title: 通过LLM增强的文本丰富图学习检测学术误引
zh.summary: LAGMiD通过链式思维提示和知识蒸馏，将LLM语义推理与图神经网络相结合来检测学术误引，在降低计算成本的同时识别与其引用声称相矛盾的参考文献。

fr.title: Détecter les Citations Erronées sur le Web Académique par Apprentissage de Graphes Texte-Enrichis Augmenté par LLM
fr.summary: LAGMiD détecte les citations erronées académiques en combinant le raisonnement sémantique LLM avec les réseaux de neurones graphiques par encouragement de pensée chaîné et distillation des connaissances, réduisant le coût informatique tout en identifiant les références qui contredisent leurs affirmations citées.

de.title: Erkennung von Fehlzitaten im akademischen Web durch LLM-verstärkte Text-Rich-Graph-Learning
de.summary: LAGMiD erkennt akademische Fehlzitationen, indem es LLM-Semantik-Reasoning mit Graphen-Neuronalen Netzen durch Chain-of-Thought-Prompting und Knowledge Distillation kombiniert und dabei die Rechenkosten reduziert, während es Referenzen identifiziert, die ihren zitierten Behauptungen widersprechen.

es.title: Detectar Citaciones Erróneas en la Web Académica mediante Aprendizaje de Gráficos Enriquecidos con Texto Aumentado por LLM
es.summary: LAGMiD detecta citaciones erróneas académicas combinando el razonamiento semántico de LLM con redes neuronales de gráficos a través de prompting de cadena de pensamiento y destilación de conocimiento, reduciendo el costo computacional mientras identifica referencias que contradicen sus afirmaciones citadas.

title: Synthetic Data Generation for Brain-Computer Interfaces: Overview, Benchmarking, and Future Directions
summary: A comprehensive survey reviews synthetic brain signal generation methods for BCIs across four algorithmic approaches, benchmarking performance across paradigms to address data scarcity and heterogeneity limiting brain-computer interface development.
content:
arXiv:2603.12296v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Deep learning has achieved transformative performance across diverse domains, largely driven by the large-scale, high-quality training data. In contrast, the development of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) is fundamentally constrained by the limited, heterogeneous, and privacy-sensitive neural recordings. Generating synthetic yet physiologically plausible brain signals has therefore emerged as a compelling way to mitigate data scarcity and enhance model capacity. This survey provides a comprehensive review of brain signal generation for BCIs, covering methodological taxonomies, benchmark experiments, evaluation metrics, and key applications. We systematically categorize existing generative algorithms into four types: knowledge-based, feature-based, model-based, and translation-based approaches. Furthermore, we benchmark existing brain signal generation approaches across four representative BCI paradigms to provide an objective performance comparison. Finally, we discuss the potentials and challenges of current generation approaches and prospect future research on accurate, data-efficient, and privacy-aware BCI systems. The benchmark codebase is publicized at https://github.com/wzwvv/DG4BCI.

zh.title: 脑机接口的合成数据生成：概述、基准测试和未来方向
zh.summary: 一项全面调查审查了脑机接口合成脑信号生成方法，跨越四种算法方法进行基准测试，以解决限制脑机接口发展的数据稀缺性和异质性问题。

fr.title: Génération de Données Synthétiques pour les Interfaces Cerveau-Ordinateur : Aperçu, Analyse Comparative et Directions Futures
fr.summary: Un sondage complet examine les méthodes de génération de signaux cérébraux synthétiques pour les interfaces cerveau-ordinateur à travers quatre approches algorithmiques, en testant les performances sur différents paradigmes pour résoudre la rareté des données et l'hétérogénéité limitant le développement des interfaces cerveau-ordinateur.

de.title: Synthetische Datenerzeugung für Gehirn-Computer-Schnittstellen: Übersicht, Benchmarking und zukünftige Richtungen
de.summary: Eine umfassende Umfrage überprüft Methoden zur Erzeugung synthetischer Gehirnsignale für Gehirn-Computer-Schnittstellen über vier algorithmische Ansätze hinweg, Benchmarking-Leistung über Paradigmen hinweg, um Datenmangel und Heterogenität zu beheben, die die Entwicklung von Gehirn-Computer-Schnittstellen einschränken.

es.title: Generación de Datos Sintéticos para Interfaces Cerebro-Computadora: Descripción General, Evaluación Comparativa y Direcciones Futuras
es.summary: Una encuesta completa revisa métodos de generación de señales cerebrales sintéticas para interfaces cerebro-computadora en cuatro enfoques algorítmicos, evaluando el rendimiento en paradigmas para abordar la escasez de datos y la heterogeneidad que limitan el desarrollo de interfaces cerebro-computadora.

title: Global Evolutionary Steering: Refining Activation Steering Control via Cross-Layer Consistency
summary: GER-steer refines activation steering in large language models by leveraging geometric stability across network layers to reduce noise and semantic drift, enabling reliable alignment without layer-specific tuning.
content:
arXiv:2603.12298v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Activation engineering enables precise control over Large Language Models (LLMs) without the computational cost of fine-tuning. However, existing methods deriving vectors from static activation differences are susceptible to high-dimensional noise and layer-wise semantic drift, often capturing spurious correlations rather than the target intent. To address this, we propose Global Evolutionary Refined Steering (GER-steer), a training-free framework that grounded in the geometric stability of the network's representation evolution. GER-steer exploits this global signal to rectify raw steering vectors, effectively decoupling robust semantic intent from orthogonal artifacts. Extensive evaluations confirm that GER-steer consistently outperforms baselines, delivering superior efficacy and generalization without layer-specific tuning, establishing a universal solution for reliable model alignment.

zh.title: 全局进化引导：通过跨层一致性优化激活引导控制
zh.summary: GER-steer通过利用网络层间的几何稳定性来完善大型语言模型中的激活引导，减少噪声和语义漂移，无需层特定调优即可实现可靠的对齐。

fr.title: Pilotage Évolutif Global : Affinage du Contrôle de Direction d'Activation via Cohérence Multicouche
fr.summary: GER-steer affine le pilotage d'activation dans les grands modèles de langage en exploitant la stabilité géométrique à travers les couches de réseau pour réduire le bruit et la dérive sémantique, permettant un alignement fiable sans réglage spécifique aux couches.

de.title: Globale Evolutionäre Lenkung: Verbesserung der Aktivierungslenkungskontrolle durch schichtenübergreifende Konsistenz
de.summary: GER-steer verfeinert die Aktivierungslenkung in großen Sprachmodellen durch Nutzung der geometrischen Stabilität über Netzwerkschichten hinweg, um Rauschen und semantische Verschiebung zu reduzieren und zuverlässige Ausrichtung ohne schichtspezifische Anpassung zu ermöglichen.

es.title: Dirección Evolutiva Global: Refinamiento del Control de Dirección de Activación a través de Consistencia Multicapa
es.summary: GER-steer refina la dirección de activación en grandes modelos de lenguaje aprovechando la estabilidad geométrica en las capas de la red para reducir el ruido y la desviación semántica, permitiendo alineación confiable sin sintonización específica de capas.

title: A Geometrically-Grounded Drive for MDL-Based Optimization in Deep Learning
summary: A novel framework integrates Minimum Description Length as an active optimization force in neural network training using coupled Ricci flow, with theoretical guarantees for representation compression and empirical validation on regression and classification tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.12304v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This paper introduces a novel optimization framework that fundamentally integrates the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle into the training dynamics of deep neural networks. Moving beyond its conventional role as a model selection criterion, we reformulate MDL as an active, adaptive driving force within the optimization process itself. The core of our method is a geometrically-grounded cognitive manifold whose evolution is governed by a \textit{coupled Ricci flow}, enriched with a novel \textit{MDL Drive} term derived from first principles. This drive, modulated by the task-loss gradient, creates a seamless harmony between data fidelity and model simplification, actively compressing the internal representation during training. We establish a comprehensive theoretical foundation, proving key properties including the monotonic decrease of description length (Theorem~\ref{thm:convergence}), a finite number of topological phase transitions via a geometric surgery protocol (Theorems~\ref{thm:surgery}, \ref{thm:ultimate_fate}), and the emergence of universal critical behavior (Theorem~\ref{thm:universality}). Furthermore, we provide a practical, computationally efficient algorithm with $O(N \log N)$ per-iteration complexity (Theorem~\ref{thm:complexity}), alongside guarantees for numerical stability (Theorem~\ref{thm:stability}) and exponential convergence under convexity assumptions (Theorem~\ref{thm:convergence_rate}). Empirical validation on synthetic regression and classification tasks confirms the theoretical predictions, demonstrating the algorithm's efficacy in achieving robust generalization and autonomous model simplification. This work provides a principled path toward more autonomous, generalizable, and interpretable AI systems by unifying geometric deep learning with information-theoretic principles.

zh.title: 深度学习中基于几何基础的MDL优化驱动
zh.summary: 一个新颖的框架将最小描述长度作为神经网络训练中的活跃优化力量，使用耦合里奇流，具有表示压缩的理论保证，以及在回归和分类任务上的实证验证。

fr.title: Un Moteur Géométriquement Fondé pour l'Optimisation Basée sur MDL en Apprentissage Profond
fr.summary: Un cadre novateur intègre la Longueur de Description Minimale comme force d'optimisation active dans la formation des réseaux de neurones en utilisant le flux de Ricci couplé, avec des garanties théoriques pour la compression de représentation et la validation empirique sur les tâches de régression et de classification.

de.title: Ein geometrisch gegründeter Antrieb für MDL-basierte Optimierung im Deep Learning
de.summary: Ein neuartiger Rahmen integriert die Mindestbeschreibungslänge als aktive Optimierungskraft beim Training neuronaler Netze unter Verwendung gekoppelter Ricci-Fluss, mit theoretischen Garantien für die Repräsentationskompression und empirische Validierung auf Regressions- und Klassifizierungsaufgaben.

es.title: Una Conducción Geométricamente Fundamentada para Optimización Basada en MDL en Aprendizaje Profundo
es.summary: Un marco novedoso integra la Longitud de Descripción Mínima como una fuerza de optimización activa en el entrenamiento de redes neuronales utilizando flujo de Ricci acoplado, con garantías teóricas para compresión de representación y validación empírica en tareas de regresión y clasificación.

title: HCP-DCNet: A Hierarchical Causal Primitive Dynamic Composition Network for Self-Improving Causal Understanding
summary: HCP-DCNet proposes a hierarchical framework decomposing causal scenes into reusable primitives with dynamic composition for task-specific reasoning, incorporating self-improvement through causal-intervention-driven meta-evolution.
content:
arXiv:2603.12305v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The ability to understand and reason about cause and effect -- encompassing interventions, counterfactuals, and underlying mechanisms -- is a cornerstone of robust artificial intelligence. While deep learning excels at pattern recognition, it fundamentally lacks a model of causality, making systems brittle under distribution shifts and unable to answer ``what-if'' questions. This paper introduces the \emph{Hierarchical Causal Primitive Dynamic Composition Network (HCP-DCNet)}, a unified framework that bridges continuous physical dynamics with discrete symbolic causal inference. Departing from monolithic representations, HCP-DCNet decomposes causal scenes into reusable, typed \emph{causal primitives} organized into four abstraction layers: physical, functional, event, and rule. A dual-channel routing network dynamically composes these primitives into task-specific, fully differentiable \emph{Causal Execution Graphs (CEGs)}. Crucially, the system employs a \emph{causal-intervention-driven meta-evolution} strategy, enabling autonomous self-improvement through a constrained Markov decision process. We establish rigorous theoretical guarantees, including type-safe composition, routing convergence, and universal approximation of causal dynamics. Extensive experiments across simulated physical and social environments demonstrate that HCP-DCNet significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in causal discovery, counterfactual reasoning, and compositional generalization. This work provides a principled, scalable, and interpretable architecture for building AI systems with human-like causal abstraction and continual self-refinement capabilities.

zh.title: HCP-DCNet：用于自我改进因果理解的分层因果原始动态组合网络
zh.summary: HCP-DCNet提出一个分层框架，将因果场景分解为可重用的原始元素，具有任务特定推理的动态组合，并通过因果干预驱动的元演化纳入自我改进。

fr.title: HCP-DCNet : Un Réseau de Composition Dynamique de Primitives Causales Hiérarchiques pour la Compréhension Causale Auto-Améliorante
fr.summary: HCP-DCNet propose un cadre hiérarchique décomposant les scènes causales en primitives réutilisables avec composition dynamique pour un raisonnement spécifique à la tâche, incorporant l'auto-amélioration par méta-évolution dirigée par interventions causales.

de.title: HCP-DCNet: Ein hierarchisches Netzwerk mit dynamischer Komposition kausaler Primitive zur selbstverbessernden kausalen Verständnis
de.summary: HCP-DCNet schlägt einen hierarchischen Rahmen vor, der kausale Szenen in wiederverwendbare Primitive mit dynamischer Komposition für aufgabenspezifisches Denken zerlegt und Selbstverbesserung durch kausale Interventions-gesteuerte Meta-Evolution einbezieht.

es.title: HCP-DCNet: Una Red Jerárquica de Primitivos Causales con Composición Dinámica para Comprensión Causal Automejorable
es.summary: HCP-DCNet propone un marco jerárquico que descompone escenas causales en primitivos reutilizables con composición dinámica para razonamiento específico de la tarea, incorporando auto-mejora a través de meta-evolución impulsada por intervención causal.

title: VQQA: An Agentic Approach for Video Evaluation and Quality Improvement
summary: VQQA introduces a multi-agent framework that improves video generation quality by replacing passive evaluation metrics with human-interpretable feedback from Vision-Language Models. The approach achieves 11.57% improvement on text-to-video benchmarks through iterative refinement without requiring model access.
content:
arXiv:2603.12310v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite rapid advancements in video generation models, aligning their outputs with complex user intent remains challenging. Existing test-time optimization methods are typically either computationally expensive or require white-box access to model internals. To address this, we present VQQA (Video Quality Question Answering), a unified, multi-agent framework generalizable across diverse input modalities and video generation tasks. By dynamically generating visual questions and using the resulting Vision-Language Model (VLM) critiques as semantic gradients, VQQA replaces traditional, passive evaluation metrics with human-interpretable, actionable feedback. This enables a highly efficient, closed-loop prompt optimization process via a black-box natural language interface. Extensive experiments demonstrate that VQQA effectively isolates and resolves visual artifacts, substantially improving generation quality in just a few refinement steps. Applicable to both text-to-video (T2V) and image-to-video (I2V) tasks, our method achieves absolute improvements of +11.57% on T2V-CompBench and +8.43% on VBench2 over vanilla generation, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art stochastic search and prompt optimization techniques.

zh.title: VQQA：视频评估和质量改进的代理方法
zh.summary: VQQA引入了一个多智能体框架，通过用来自视觉语言模型的人类可解释反馈替换被动评估指标来改进视频生成质量。该方法在文本到视频基准上实现了11.57%的改进，通过迭代细化，无需访问模型。

fr.title: VQQA : Une approche agentique pour l'évaluation vidéo et l'amélioration de la qualité
fr.summary: VQQA introduit un framework multi-agents qui améliore la qualité de génération vidéo en remplaçant les métriques d'évaluation passives par des retours interprétables par les humains provenant de modèles Vision-Langage. L'approche atteint une amélioration de 11,57% sur les benchmarks texte-vidéo par raffinement itératif sans nécessiter l'accès au modèle.

de.title: VQQA: Ein agentischer Ansatz zur Videobewertung und Qualitätsverbesserung
de.summary: VQQA führt ein Multi-Agent-Framework ein, das die Qualität der Videogenerierung verbessert, indem passive Evaluierungsmetriken durch menschlich interpretierbare Rückmeldungen von Vision-Language-Modellen ersetzt werden. Der Ansatz erreicht eine Verbesserung von 11,57% in Text-zu-Video-Benchmarks durch iterative Verfeinerung ohne Modellzugriff.

es.title: VQQA: Un enfoque agentivo para la evaluación y mejora de calidad de video
es.summary: VQQA introduce un marco multiagente que mejora la calidad de generación de video reemplazando métricas de evaluación pasivas con retroalimentación interpretable por humanos de modelos Vision-Language. El enfoque logra una mejora del 11,57% en puntos de referencia texto-a-video mediante refinamiento iterativo sin requerir acceso al modelo.

title: Thermodynamics of Reinforcement Learning Curricula
summary: Researchers connect statistical mechanics and curriculum learning in reinforcement learning, proposing that optimal training curricula correspond to geodesics in task space. The MEW algorithm provides principled temperature annealing schedules grounded in non-equilibrium thermodynamics.
content:
arXiv:2603.12324v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Connections between statistical mechanics and machine learning have repeatedly proven fruitful, providing insight into optimization, generalization, and representation learning. In this work, we follow this tradition by leveraging results from non-equilibrium thermodynamics to formalize curriculum learning in reinforcement learning (RL). In particular, we propose a geometric framework for RL by interpreting reward parameters as coordinates on a task manifold. We show that, by minimizing the excess thermodynamic work, optimal curricula correspond to geodesics in this task space. As an application of this framework, we provide an algorithm, "MEW" (Minimum Excess Work), to derive a principled schedule for temperature annealing in maximum-entropy RL.

zh.title: 强化学习课程的热力学
zh.summary: 研究人员将统计力学与强化学习中的课程学习联系起来，提出最优训练课程对应于任务空间中的测地线。MEW算法基于非平衡热力学提供有原则的温度退火调度。

fr.title: Thermodynamique des curriculums d'apprentissage par renforcement
fr.summary: Les chercheurs connectent la mécanique statistique et l'apprentissage par curriculum en apprentissage par renforcement, proposant que les curriculums d'entraînement optimaux correspondent à des géodésiques dans l'espace des tâches. L'algorithme MEW fournit des calendriers d'recuit de température fondés sur la thermodynamique hors équilibre.

de.title: Thermodynamik von Reinforcement-Learning-Curricula
de.summary: Forscher verbinden statistische Mechanik und Curriculum-Learning im Reinforcement Learning und schlagen vor, dass optimale Trainings-Curricula Geodäten im Aufgabenraum entsprechen. Der MEW-Algorithmus bietet prinzipielle Temperaturabkühlungspläne, die in der Nichtgleichgewichtsthermodynamik verankert sind.

es.title: Termodinámica de currículos de aprendizaje por refuerzo
es.summary: Los investigadores conectan la mecánica estadística y el aprendizaje por currículo en el aprendizaje por refuerzo, proponiendo que los currículos de entrenamiento óptimos corresponden a geodésicas en el espacio de tareas. El algoritmo MEW proporciona cronogramas de temple de temperatura fundamentados en la termodinámica fuera del equilibrio.

title: Maximum Entropy Exploration Without the Rollouts
summary: EVE (EigenVector-based Exploration) algorithm solves maximum entropy exploration without expensive rollouts by computing stationary distributions from transition matrix eigenvectors. The approach achieves competitive exploration performance while reducing computational costs significantly.
content:
arXiv:2603.12325v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Efficient exploration remains a central challenge in reinforcement learning, serving as a useful pretraining objective for data collection, particularly when an external reward function is unavailable. A principled formulation of the exploration problem is to find policies that maximize the entropy of their induced steady-state visitation distribution, thereby encouraging uniform long-run coverage of the state space. Many existing exploration approaches require estimating state visitation frequencies through repeated on-policy rollouts, which can be computationally expensive. In this work, we instead consider an intrinsic average-reward formulation in which the reward is derived from the visitation distribution itself, so that the optimal policy maximizes steady-state entropy. An entropy-regularized version of this objective admits a spectral characterization: the relevant stationary distributions can be computed from the dominant eigenvectors of a problem-dependent transition matrix. This insight leads to a novel algorithm for solving the maximum entropy exploration problem, EVE (EigenVector-based Exploration), which avoids explicit rollouts and distribution estimation, instead computing the solution through iterative updates, similar to a value-based approach. To address the original unregularized objective, we employ a posterior-policy iteration (PPI) approach, which monotonically improves the entropy and converges in value. We prove convergence of EVE under standard assumptions and demonstrate empirically that it efficiently produces policies with high steady-state entropy, achieving competitive exploration performance relative to rollout-based baselines in deterministic grid-world environments.

zh.title: 无需轨迹的最大熵探索
zh.summary: EVE（基于特征向量的探索）算法通过计算转移矩阵特征向量的平稳分布来解决最大熵探索问题，无需昂贵的轨迹回滚。该方法在保持竞争性探索性能的同时显著降低计算成本。

fr.title: Exploration d'entropie maximale sans rollouts
fr.summary: L'algorithme EVE (Exploration basée sur les vecteurs propres) résout l'exploration d'entropie maximale sans rollouts coûteux en calculant les distributions stationnaires à partir des vecteurs propres de la matrice de transition. L'approche atteint des performances d'exploration compétitives tout en réduisant considérablement les coûts informatiques.

de.title: Maximale Entropie-Exploration ohne Rollouts
de.summary: Der EVE-Algorithmus (EigenVector-basierte Exploration) löst die Exploration maximaler Entropie ohne teure Rollouts, indem er stationäre Verteilungen aus Eigenvektoren der Übergangsmatrix berechnet. Der Ansatz erreicht wettbewerbsfähige Explorationsleistung bei gleichzeitiger signifikanter Reduzierung der Rechenkosten.

es.title: Exploración de entropía máxima sin rollouts
es.summary: El algoritmo EVE (Exploración basada en vectores propios) resuelve la exploración de entropía máxima sin rollouts costosos calculando distribuciones estacionarias a partir de vectores propios de la matriz de transición. El enfoque logra un desempeño de exploración competitivo mientras reduce significativamente los costos computacionales.

title: Optimizing Task Completion Time Updates Using POMDPs
summary: This work formulates task announcement management as a POMDP to optimize when and how to update stakeholders about project completion times. The framework balances announcement accuracy against update frequency costs, achieving up to 75% reduction in unnecessary timeline updates.
content:
arXiv:2603.12340v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Managing announced task completion times is a fundamental control problem in project management. While extensive research exists on estimating task durations and task scheduling, the problem of when and how to update completion times communicated to stakeholders remains understudied. Organizations must balance announcement accuracy against the costs of frequent timeline updates, which can erode stakeholder trust and trigger costly replanning. Despite the prevalence of this problem, current approaches rely on static predictions or ad-hoc policies that fail to account for the sequential nature of announcement management. In this paper, we formulate the task announcement problem as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP) where the control policy must decide when to update announced completion times based on noisy observations of true task completion. Since most state variables (current time and previous announcements) are fully observable, we leverage the Mixed Observability MDP (MOMDP) framework to enable more efficient policy optimization. Our reward structure captures the dual costs of announcement errors and update frequency, enabling synthesis of optimal announcement control policies. Using off-the-shelf solvers, we generate policies that act as feedback controllers, adaptively managing announcements based on belief state evolution. Simulation results demonstrate significant improvements in both accuracy and announcement stability compared to baseline strategies, achieving up to 75\% reduction in unnecessary updates while maintaining or improving prediction accuracy.

zh.title: 使用POMDP优化任务完成时间更新
zh.summary: 本工作将任务公告管理表述为POMDP,以优化何时以及如何向利益相关者更新项目完成时间。该框架在公告准确性和更新频率成本之间取得平衡,可实现75%的不必要时间表更新减少。

fr.title: Optimisation des Mises à Jour de Délai d'Exécution des Tâches à l'aide de POMDP
fr.summary: Ce travail formule la gestion des annonces de tâches comme un POMDP pour optimiser quand et comment mettre à jour les parties prenantes sur les délais d'exécution du projet. Le cadre équilibre la précision des annonces par rapport aux coûts de fréquence de mise à jour, réalisant une réduction jusqu'à 75% des mises à jour de calendrier inutiles.

de.title: Optimierung von Aufgabenabschlusszeit-Updates mit POMDPs
de.summary: Diese Arbeit formuliert Aufgabenmitteilungsverwaltung als POMDP, um zu optimieren, wann und wie Stakeholder über Projektabschlusszeiten aktualisiert werden. Das Framework balanciert Mitteilungsgenauigkeit gegen Kosten der Aktualisierungshäufigkeit und erreicht eine Reduzierung um bis zu 75% unnötiger Zeitplanaktualisierungen.

es.title: Optimización de Actualizaciones de Tiempo de Finalización de Tareas Usando POMDPs
es.summary: Este trabajo formula la gestión de anuncios de tareas como un POMDP para optimizar cuándo y cómo actualizar a las partes interesadas sobre los tiempos de finalización del proyecto. El marco equilibra la precisión del anuncio frente a los costos de frecuencia de actualización, logrando una reducción de hasta el 75% en actualizaciones de cronograma innecesarias.

title: Budget-Sensitive Discovery Scoring: A Formally Verified Framework for Evaluating AI-Guided Scientific Selection
summary: BSDS introduces a formally verified metric (machine-checked by Lean 4) for evaluating AI-guided scientific candidate selection under budget constraints, penalizing both false discoveries and excessive abstention. Applying it to drug discovery reveals simple ML baselines outperform LLMs, establishing they add no marginal value for this task.
content:
arXiv:2603.12349v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Scientific discovery increasingly relies on AI systems to select candidates for expensive experimental validation, yet no principled, budget-aware evaluation framework exists for comparing selection strategies -- a gap intensified by large language models (LLMs), which generate plausible scientific proposals without reliable downstream evaluation. We introduce the Budget-Sensitive Discovery Score (BSDS), a formally verified metric -- 20 theorems machine-checked by the Lean 4 proof assistant -- that jointly penalizes false discoveries (lambda-weighted FDR) and excessive abstention (gamma-weighted coverage gap) at each budget level. Its budget-averaged form, the Discovery Quality Score (DQS), provides a single summary statistic that no proposer can inflate by performing well at a cherry-picked budget.
  As a case study, we apply BSDS/DQS to: do LLMs add marginal value to an existing ML pipeline for drug discovery candidate selection? We evaluate 39 proposers -- 11 mechanistic variants, 14 zero-shot LLM configurations, and 14 few-shot LLM configurations -- using SMILES representations on MoleculeNet HIV (41,127 compounds, 3.5% active, 1,000 bootstrap replicates) under both random and scaffold splits. Three findings emerge. First, the simple RF-based Greedy-ML proposer achieves the best DQS (-0.046), outperforming all MLP variants and LLM configurations. Second, no LLM surpasses the Greedy-ML baseline under zero-shot or few-shot evaluation on HIV or Tox21, establishing that LLMs provide no marginal value over an existing trained classifier. Third, the proposer hierarchy generalizes across five MoleculeNet benchmarks spanning 0.18%-46.2% prevalence, a non-drug AV safety domain, and a 9x7 grid of penalty parameters (tau >= 0.636, mean tau = 0.863). The framework applies to any setting where candidates are selected under budget constraints and asymmetric error costs.

zh.title: 预算敏感发现评分:用于评估AI引导科学选择的形式验证框架
zh.summary: BSDS引入了一个形式验证的指标(通过Lean 4机器检查)来评估在预算约束下的AI引导科学候选人选择,对假发现和过度弃权都进行惩罚。将其应用于药物发现表明简单的机器学习基线优于大型语言模型,证明它们对这项任务没有边际价值。

fr.title: Notation de Découverte Sensible au Budget: Un Cadre Formellement Vérifié pour Évaluer la Sélection Scientifique Guidée par l'IA
fr.summary: BSDS introduit une métrique formellement vérifiée (vérifiée par machine par Lean 4) pour évaluer la sélection de candidats scientifiques guidée par l'IA sous des contraintes budgétaires, pénalisant à la fois les fausses découvertes et l'abstention excessive. Son application à la découverte de médicaments révèle que les simples référentiels ML surpassent les LLM, établissant qu'ils n'ajoutent aucune valeur marginale pour cette tâche.

de.title: Budgetempfindliche Discovery-Bewertung: Ein formal verifizierten Framework zur Bewertung der KI-geführten wissenschaftlichen Auswahl
de.summary: BSDS führt eine formell verifizierten Metrik (maschinengeprüft durch Lean 4) zur Bewertung der KI-geführten wissenschaftlichen Kandidatenauswahl unter Budgetbeschränkungen ein, die sowohl falsche Entdeckungen als auch excessive Enthaltung bestraft. Die Anwendung auf die Arzneimittelentdeckung zeigt, dass einfache ML-Baselines LLMs übertreffen, was etabliert, dass sie für diese Aufgabe keinen zusätzlichen Wert hinzufügen.

es.title: Puntuación de Descubrimiento Sensible al Presupuesto: Un Marco Formalmente Verificado para Evaluar la Selección Científica Guiada por IA
es.summary: BSDS introduce una métrica formalmente verificada (verificada por máquina por Lean 4) para evaluar la selección de candidatos científicos guiada por IA bajo restricciones presupuestarias, penalizando tanto los falsos descubrimientos como la abstención excesiva. Al aplicarlo al descubrimiento de fármacos, se revela que los simples conjuntos de referencia de ML superan a los LLM, estableciendo que no añaden ningún valor marginal para esta tarea.

title: SPARROW: Learning Spatial Precision and Temporal Referential Consistency in Pixel-Grounded Video MLLMs
summary: SPARROW advances pixel-grounded video understanding by ensuring spatial precision and temporal consistency through tracked feature injection and dual-prompt design. Integrated into existing video MLLMs, it achieves gains up to +8.9 J&F on referential video segmentation and +5 mIoU on visual grounding.
content:
arXiv:2603.12382v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have advanced from image-level reasoning to pixel-level grounding, but extending these capabilities to videos remains challenging as models must achieve spatial precision and temporally consistent reference tracking. Existing video MLLMs often rely on a static segmentation token ([SEG]) for frame-wise grounding, which provides semantics but lacks temporal context, causing spatial drift, identity switches, and unstable initialization when objects move or reappear. We introduce SPARROW, a pixel-grounded video MLLM that unifies spatial accuracy and temporal stability through two key components: (i) Target-Specific Tracked Features (TSF), which inject temporally aligned referent cues during training, and (ii) a dual-prompt design that decodes box ([BOX]) and segmentation ([SEG]) tokens to fuse geometric priors with semantic grounding. SPARROW is supported by a curated referential video dataset of 30,646 videos and 45,231 Q&A pairs and operates end-to-end without external detectors via a class-agnostic SAM2-based proposer. Integrated into three recent open-source video MLLMs (UniPixel, GLUS, and VideoGLaMM), SPARROW delivers consistent gains across six benchmarks, improving up to +8.9 J&F on RVOS, +5 mIoU on visual grounding, and +5.4 CLAIR on GCG. These results demonstrate that SPARROW substantially improves referential stability, spatial precision, and temporal coherence in pixel-grounded video understanding. Project page: https://risys-lab.github.io/SPARROW

zh.title: SPARROW: 学习像素锚定视频多模态大模型中的空间精度和时间参考一致性
zh.summary: SPARROW通过跟踪特征注入和双提示设计来确保空间精度和时间一致性,从而推进像素锚定视频理解。集成到现有视频多模态大模型中,它在参考视频分割上实现了高达+8.9 J&F的增益,在视觉定位上实现了+5 mIoU的增益。

fr.title: SPARROW: Apprentissage de la Précision Spatiale et de la Cohérence Référentielle Temporelle dans les MLLMs Vidéo Ancrés au Pixel
fr.summary: SPARROW fait progresser la compréhension vidéo ancrée au pixel en garantissant la précision spatiale et la cohérence temporelle grâce à l'injection de caractéristiques suivies et à la conception à double invite. Intégré dans les MLLMs vidéo existants, il réalise des gains jusqu'à +8,9 J&F sur la segmentation vidéo référentielle et +5 mIoU sur l'ancrage visuel.

de.title: SPARROW: Erlernen von räumlicher Präzision und zeitlicher Referenzübereinstimmung in pixelverankerten Video-MLLMs
de.summary: SPARROW erweitert das pixelverankertes Videoverständnis durch Gewährleistung von räumlicher Präzision und zeitlicher Konsistenz durch verfolgte Merkmalsinjektionen und duales Prompt-Design. Integriert in bestehende Video-MLLMs erreicht es Gewinne von bis zu +8,9 J&F bei referenzieller Videosegmentierung und +5 mIoU bei visueller Verankerung.

es.title: SPARROW: Aprendizaje de Precisión Espacial y Coherencia Referencial Temporal en MLLMs de Video Anclados en Píxeles
es.summary: SPARROW avanza en la comprensión de video anclado en píxeles asegurando precisión espacial y consistencia temporal mediante inyección de características rastreadas y diseño de doble solicitud. Integrado en MLLMs de video existentes, logra ganancias de hasta +8.9 J&F en segmentación de video referencial y +5 mIoU en anclaje visual.

title: Test-Time Strategies for More Efficient and Accurate Agentic RAG
summary: Contextualization and de-duplication modules improve agentic RAG systems for multi-hop questions, increasing exact match accuracy by 5.6% and reducing retrieval turns by 10.5% compared to baseline approaches. The optimizations address inefficiencies from repetitive document retrieval and suboptimal reasoning integration.
content:
arXiv:2603.12396v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems face challenges with complex, multihop questions, and agentic frameworks such as Search-R1 (Jin et al., 2025), which operates iteratively, have been proposed to address these complexities. However, such approaches can introduce inefficiencies, including repetitive retrieval of previously processed information and challenges in contextualizing retrieved results effectively within the current generation prompt. Such issues can lead to unnecessary retrieval turns, suboptimal reasoning, inaccurate answers, and increased token consumption.
  In this paper, we investigate test-time modifications to the Search-R1 pipeline to mitigate these identified shortcomings. Specifically, we explore the integration of two components and their combination: a contextualization module to better integrate relevant information from retrieved documents into reasoning, and a de-duplication module that replaces previously retrieved documents with the next most relevant ones. We evaluate our approaches using the HotpotQA (Yang et al., 2018) and the Natural Questions (Kwiatkowski et al., 2019) datasets, reporting the exact match (EM) score, an LLM-as-a-Judge assessment of answer correctness, and the average number of turns.
  Our best-performing variant, utilizing GPT-4.1-mini for contextualization, achieves a 5.6% increase in EM score and reduces the number of turns by 10.5% compared to the Search-R1 baseline, demonstrating improved answer accuracy and retrieval efficiency.

zh.title: 测试时间策略提高代理RAG的效率和准确性
zh.summary: 上下文化和去重模块改进了多跳问题的代理RAG系统，相比基线方法提高精确匹配准确率5.6%，减少检索轮次10.5%。这些优化解决了重复文档检索和次优推理整合带来的低效问题。

fr.title: Stratégies au moment du test pour une RAG agentique plus efficace et précise
fr.summary: Les modules de contextualisation et de déduplication améliorent les systèmes RAG agentiques pour les questions multi-sauts, augmentant la précision des correspondances exactes de 5,6% et réduisant les tours de récupération de 10,5% par rapport aux approches de base. Les optimisations résolvent les inefficacités dues à la récupération répétitive de documents et à l'intégration de raisonnement sous-optimal.

de.title: Test-Zeit-Strategien für effizientere und genauere agentische RAG
de.summary: Kontextualisierungs- und Deduplizierungsmodule verbessern agentische RAG-Systeme für Multi-Hop-Fragen, erhöhen die Genauigkeit der exakten Übereinstimmung um 5,6% und verringern die Abrufvorgänge um 10,5% im Vergleich zu Baseline-Ansätzen. Die Optimierungen beheben Ineffizienzen durch wiederholte Dokumentabrufe und suboptimale Integrationszusammenhänge.

es.title: Estrategias de tiempo de prueba para RAG agentivo más eficiente y preciso
es.summary: Los módulos de contextualización y deduplicación mejoran los sistemas RAG agentivos para preguntas multi-salto, aumentando la precisión de coincidencia exacta en un 5,6% y reduciendo los turnos de recuperación en un 10,5% en comparación con enfoques de referencia. Las optimizaciones abordan ineficiencias por recuperación repetida de documentos e integración de razonamiento subóptima.

title: Revisiting Model Stitching In the Foundation Model Era
summary: Model stitching—connecting early layers of one foundation model to later layers of another—becomes practical for heterogeneous vision models with proper stitch layer training. The VFM Stitch Tree enables multimodal systems to leverage complementary foundation model strengths with minimal inference overhead.
content:
arXiv:2603.12433v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Model stitching, connecting early layers of one model (source) to later layers of another (target) via a light stitch layer, has served as a probe of representational compatibility. Prior work finds that models trained on the same dataset remain stitchable (negligible accuracy drop) despite different initializations or objectives. We revisit stitching for Vision Foundation Models (VFMs) that vary in objectives, data, and modality mix (e.g., CLIP, DINOv2, SigLIP 2) and ask: Are heterogeneous VFMs stitchable? We introduce a systematic protocol spanning the stitch points, stitch layer families, training losses, and downstream tasks. Three findings emerge. (1) Stitch layer training matters: conventional approaches that match the intermediate features at the stitch point or optimize the task loss end-to-end struggle to retain accuracy, especially at shallow stitch points. (2) With a simple feature-matching loss at the target model's penultimate layer, heterogeneous VFMs become reliably stitchable across vision tasks. (3) For deep stitch points, the stitched model can surpass either constituent model at only a small inference overhead (for the stitch layer). Building on these findings, we further propose the VFM Stitch Tree (VST), which shares early layers across VFMs while retaining their later layers, yielding a controllable accuracy-latency trade-off for multimodal LLMs that often leverage multiple VFMs. Taken together, our study elevates stitching from a diagnostic probe to a practical recipe for integrating complementary VFM strengths and pinpointing where their representations align or diverge.

zh.title: 重新审视基础模型时代的模型缝合
zh.summary: 模型缝合——连接一个基础模型的早期层与另一个模型的后期层——通过适当的缝合层训练对异构视觉模型变得可行。VFM缝合树使多模态系统能以最小推理开销利用互补的基础模型强度。

fr.title: Revisiter l'assemblage de modèles à l'ère des modèles fondamentaux
fr.summary: L'assemblage de modèles—connectant les couches précoces d'un modèle fondamental aux couches tardives d'un autre—devient pratique pour les modèles de vision hétérogènes avec un entraînement approprié de la couche d'assemblage. L'arbre VFM Stitch permet aux systèmes multimodaux de tirer parti des forces complémentaires des modèles fondamentaux avec un surcoût d'inférence minimal.

de.title: Modellverknüpfung im Zeitalter von Stiftungsmodellen neu bewerten
de.summary: Modellverknüpfung—Verbindung früher Schichten eines Stiftungsmodells mit späteren Schichten eines anderen—wird für heterogene Visionmodelle mit ordnungsgemäßem Training der Verknüpfungsschicht praktisch. Der VFM Stitch Tree ermöglicht es multimodalen Systemen, komplementäre Stiftungsmodellstärken mit minimalem Inferenzaufwand zu nutzen.

es.title: Revisando el ensamblaje de modelos en la era de los modelos fundamentales
es.summary: El ensamblaje de modelos—conectar capas tempranas de un modelo fundamental a capas posteriores de otro—se vuelve práctico para modelos de visión heterogéneos con entrenamiento apropiado de la capa de empalme. El árbol VFM Stitch permite que los sistemas multimodales aprovechen las fortalezas complementarias de los modelos fundamentales con sobrecarga de inferencia mínima.

title: Unmasking Biases and Reliability Concerns in Convolutional Neural Networks Analysis of Cancer Pathology Images
summary: CNNs achieve unexpectedly high accuracy (up to 93%) classifying background crops from cancer pathology images lacking clinical content, exposing fundamental biases in standard evaluation practices. The findings challenge the reliability of benchmark datasets and highlight the need for more rigorous validation in medical AI.
content:
arXiv:2603.12445v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Convolutional Neural Networks have shown promising effectiveness in identifying different types of cancer from radiographs. However, the opaque nature of CNNs makes it difficult to fully understand the way they operate, limiting their assessment to empirical evaluation. Here we study the soundness of the standard practices by which CNNs are evaluated for the purpose of cancer pathology. Thirteen highly used cancer benchmark datasets were analyzed, using four common CNN architectures and different types of cancer, such as melanoma, carcinoma, colorectal cancer, and lung cancer. We compared the accuracy of each model with that of datasets made of cropped segments from the background of the original images that do not contain clinically relevant content. Because the rendered datasets contain no clinical information, the null hypothesis is that the CNNs should provide mere chance-based accuracy when classifying these datasets. The results show that the CNN models provided high accuracy when using the cropped segments, sometimes as high as 93\%, even though they lacked biomedical information. These results show that some CNN architectures are more sensitive to bias than others. The analysis shows that the common practices of machine learning evaluation might lead to unreliable results when applied to cancer pathology. These biases are very difficult to identify, and might mislead researchers as they use available benchmark datasets to test the efficacy of CNN methods.

zh.title: 揭示卷积神经网络分析癌症病理图像中的偏见和可靠性问题
zh.summary: CNN在对缺乏临床内容的癌症病理图像背景裁剪进行分类时达到意外高准确率（高达93%），暴露了标准评估实践中的根本偏见。这些发现质疑基准数据集的可靠性，突出了医学AI中更严格验证的必要性。

fr.title: Démasquer les biais et les préoccupations en matière de fiabilité dans l'analyse des réseaux de neurones convolutifs des images de pathologie cancéreuse
fr.summary: Les CNN réalisent une précision étonnamment élevée (jusqu'à 93%) en classifiant les cultures de fond à partir d'images de pathologie cancéreuse dépourvues de contenu clinique, exposant les biais fondamentaux dans les pratiques d'évaluation standard. Les résultats remettent en question la fiabilité des ensembles de données de référence et soulignent la nécessité d'une validation plus rigoureuse en IA médicale.

de.title: Verzerrungen und Zuverlässigkeitsbedenken in der Analyse von Faltungs-Neuronalen Netzen von Krebspathologie-Bildern aufdecken
de.summary: CNNs erreichen eine überraschend hohe Genauigkeit (bis zu 93%) bei der Klassifizierung von Hintergrundzuschnitten aus Krebspathologiebildern ohne klinischen Inhalt und offenbaren grundlegende Verzerrungen in Standard-Evaluierungspraktiken. Die Ergebnisse stellen die Zuverlässigkeit von Benchmark-Datensätzen in Frage und unterstreichen die Notwendigkeit einer rigoroseren Validierung in der medizinischen KI.

es.title: Desenmascarar sesgos y preocupaciones de confiabilidad en el análisis de redes neuronales convolucionales de imágenes de patología del cáncer
es.summary: Las CNN logran una precisión sorprendentemente alta (hasta 93%) clasificando cultivos de fondo de imágenes de patología del cáncer que carecen de contenido clínico, exponiendo sesgos fundamentales en las prácticas de evaluación estándar. Los hallazgos cuestionan la confiabilidad de los conjuntos de datos de referencia y destacan la necesidad de una validación más rigurosa en IA médica.

title: Operationalising Cyber Risk Management Using AI: Connecting Cyber Incidents to MITRE ATT&CK Techniques, Security Controls, and Metrics
summary: A fine-tuned sentence-transformer model maps cyber incidents to MITRE ATT&CK techniques and CIS controls, enabling resource-constrained organizations to operationalize incident response systematically. The Cyber Catalog framework achieves 0.7894 Spearman correlation and connects threat intelligence to actionable security measures.
content:
arXiv:2603.12455v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The escalating frequency of cyber-attacks poses significant challenges for organisations, particularly small enterprises constrained by limited in-house expertise, insufficient knowledge, and financial resources. This research presents a novel framework that leverages Natural Language Processing to address these challenges through automated mapping of cyber incidents to adversary techniques. We introduce the Cyber Catalog, a knowledge base that systematically integrates CIS Critical Security Controls, MITRE ATT&CK techniques, and SMART metrics. This integrated resource enables organisations to connect threat intelligence directly to actionable controls and measurable outcomes. To operationalise the framework, we fine-tuned all-mpnet-base-v2, a highly regarded sentence-transformers model used to convert text into numerical vectors on an augmented dataset comprising 74,986 incident-technique pairs to enhance semantic similarity between cyber incidents and MITRE ATT&CK techniques. Our fine-tuned model achieved a Spearman correlation of 0.7894 and Pearson correlation of 0.8756, demonstrating substantial improvements over top baseline models including all-mpnet-base-v2, all-distilroberta-v1, and all-MiniLM-L12-v2. Furthermore, our model exhibited significantly lower prediction errors (MAE = 0.135, MSE = 0.027) compared to all baseline models, confirming superior accuracy and consistency. The Cyber Catalog, training dataset, trained model, and implementation code made publicly available to facilitate further research and enable practical deployment in resource-constrained environments. This work bridges the gap between threat intelligence and operational security management, providing an actionable tool for systematic cyber incident response and evidence-based cyber risk management.

zh.title: 利用人工智能实现网络风险管理运营化：将网络事件与MITRE ATT&CK技术、安全控制和指标相关联
zh.summary: 一个微调的句子转换器模型将网络事件映射到MITRE ATT&CK技术和CIS控制，使资源受限的组织能够系统地实现事件响应运营化。网络目录框架实现了0.7894的Spearman相关性，并将威胁情报与可操作的安全措施相关联。

fr.title: Opérationnaliser la gestion des risques cybernétiques à l'aide de l'IA : Connecter les incidents de cybersécurité aux techniques MITRE ATT&CK, aux contrôles de sécurité et aux métriques
fr.summary: Un modèle transformateur de phrases affiné mappe les incidents de cybersécurité aux techniques MITRE ATT&CK et aux contrôles CIS, permettant aux organisations aux ressources limitées d'opérationnaliser la réponse aux incidents de manière systématique. Le cadre Cyber Catalog atteint une corrélation de Spearman de 0,7894 et relie l'intelligence des menaces à des mesures de sécurité exploitables.

de.title: Operationalisierung des Cybersicherheitsrisikomanagements mit KI: Verbindung von Cybersicherheitsvorfällen mit MITRE ATT&CK-Techniken, Sicherheitskontrollen und Metriken
de.summary: Ein optimiertes Satzumwandler-Modell ordnet Cybersicherheitsvorfälle MITRE ATT&CK-Techniken und CIS-Kontrollen zu, was ressourcenbeschränkten Organisationen ermöglicht, die Vorfallreaktion systematisch zu operationalisieren. Das Cyber Catalog-Framework erreicht eine Spearman-Korrelation von 0,7894 und verbindet Threat Intelligence mit umsetzbaren Sicherheitsmaßnahmen.

es.title: Operacionalizar la gestión de riesgos cibernéticos mediante IA: Conectar incidentes cibernéticos con técnicas MITRE ATT&CK, controles de seguridad y métricas
es.summary: Un modelo transformador de oraciones ajustado asigna incidentes cibernéticos a técnicas MITRE ATT&CK y controles CIS, permitiendo que organizaciones con recursos limitados operacionalicen sistemáticamente la respuesta a incidentes. El marco Cyber Catalog logra una correlación de Spearman de 0,7894 y conecta la inteligencia de amenazas con medidas de seguridad accionables.

title: Shattering the Shortcut: A Topology-Regularized Benchmark for Multi-hop Medical Reasoning in LLMs
summary: ShatterMed-QA, a bilingual benchmark of 10,558 multi-hop clinical questions, uses topology-regularized knowledge graphs to eliminate shortcut learning in medical LLMs. Evaluation reveals significant reasoning deficits in existing models but shows RAG effectively restores performance by providing correct evidence.
content:
arXiv:2603.12458v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve expert-level performance on standard medical benchmarks through single-hop factual recall, they severely struggle with the complex, multi-hop diagnostic reasoning required in real-world clinical settings. A primary obstacle is "shortcut learning", where models exploit highly connected, generic hub nodes (e.g., "inflammation") in knowledge graphs to bypass authentic micro-pathological cascades. To address this, we introduce ShatterMed-QA, a bilingual benchmark of 10,558 multi-hop clinical questions designed to rigorously evaluate deep diagnostic reasoning. Our framework constructs a topology-regularized medical Knowledge Graph using a novel $k$-Shattering algorithm, which physically prunes generic hubs to explicitly sever logical shortcuts. We synthesize the evaluation vignettes by applying implicit bridge entity masking and topology-driven hard negative sampling, forcing models to navigate biologically plausible distractors without relying on superficial elimination. Comprehensive evaluations of 21 LLMs reveal massive performance degradation on our multi-hop tasks, particularly among domain-specific models. Crucially, restoring the masked evidence via Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) triggers near-universal performance recovery, validating ShatterMed-QA's structural fidelity and proving its efficacy in diagnosing the fundamental reasoning deficits of current medical AI. Explore the dataset, interactive examples, and full leaderboards at our project website: https://shattermed-qa-web.vercel.app/

zh.title: 打破捷径：医学大语言模型多跳推理的拓扑正则化基准
zh.summary: ShatterMed-QA是一个包含10,558个多跳临床问题的双语基准，使用拓扑正则化知识图谱来消除医学大语言模型中的捷径学习。评估揭示了现有模型中存在的显著推理缺陷，但显示RAG通过提供正确证据有效恢复了性能。

fr.title: Briser le raccourci : Un benchmark régularisé par topologie pour le raisonnement médical multi-sauts dans les LLMs
fr.summary: ShatterMed-QA, un benchmark bilingue de 10 558 questions cliniques multi-sauts, utilise des graphes de connaissances régularisés par topologie pour éliminer l'apprentissage par raccourci dans les LLMs médicaux. L'évaluation révèle des déficits de raisonnement significatifs dans les modèles existants, mais montre que RAG restaure efficacement les performances en fournissant des preuves correctes.

de.title: Den Abkürzungsweg durchbrechen: Ein topologisch regulierter Benchmark für mehrschrittige medizinische Überlegungen in LLMs
de.summary: ShatterMed-QA, ein zweisprachiger Benchmark mit 10.558 mehrschrittigen klinischen Fragen, nutzt topologisch regulierte Wissensgraphen, um abgekürztes Lernen in medizinischen LLMs zu eliminieren. Die Bewertung zeigt erhebliche Mängel beim Denken in bestehenden Modellen, aber RAG stellt die Leistung effektiv wieder her, indem es korrekte Beweise bereitstellt.

es.title: Rompiendo el atajo: Un benchmark regularizado por topología para el razonamiento médico de múltiples saltos en LLMs
es.summary: ShatterMed-QA, un benchmark bilingüe de 10,558 preguntas clínicas de múltiples saltos, utiliza gráficos de conocimiento regularizados por topología para eliminar el aprendizaje de atajos en LLMs médicos. La evaluación revela déficits significativos de razonamiento en modelos existentes, pero muestra que RAG restaura efectivamente el rendimiento al proporcionar evidencia correcta.

title: CLARE: Classification-based Regression for Electron Temperature Prediction
summary: CLARE uses classification-based regression to predict electron temperature in Earth's plasmasphere from satellite data and geomagnetic indices, achieving 69.67% accuracy within 10% of ground truth. The discretized classification approach improves prediction accuracy by 6.46% while providing uncertainty estimates.
content:
arXiv:2603.12470v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Electron temperature (Te) is an important parameter governing space weather in the upper atmosphere, but has historically been underexplored in the space weather machine learning literature. We present CLARE, a machine learning model for predicting electron temperature in the Earth's plasmasphere trained on AKEBONO (EXOS-D) satellite measurements as well as solar and geomagnetic indices. CLARE uses a classification-based regression architecture that transforms the continuous Te output space into 150 discrete classification intervals. Training the model on a classification task improves prediction accuracy by 6.46% relative compared to a traditional regression model while also outputting uncertainty estimation information on its predictions. On a held out test set from the AKEBONO data, the model's Te predictions achieve 69.67% accuracy within 10% of the ground truth and 46.17% on a known geomagnetic storm period from January 30th to February 7th, 1991. We show that machine learning can be used to produce high-accuracy Te models on publicly available data.

zh.title: CLARE：基于分类的回归用于电子温度预测
zh.summary: CLARE使用基于分类的回归从卫星数据和地磁指数预测地球等离子圈中的电子温度，达到了69.67%的精度，在地面真值的10%以内。离散化分类方法将预测精度提高了6.46%，同时提供了不确定性估计。

fr.title: CLARE : Régression basée sur la classification pour la prédiction de la température des électrons
fr.summary: CLARE utilise la régression basée sur la classification pour prédire la température des électrons dans la plasmasphère terrestre à partir des données satellites et des indices géomagnétiques, atteignant une précision de 69,67% à moins de 10% de la vérité terrain. L'approche de classification discrétisée améliore la précision de la prédiction de 6,46% tout en fournissant des estimations d'incertitude.

de.title: CLARE: Klassifikationsbasierte Regression zur Vorhersage der Elektronentemperatur
de.summary: CLARE verwendet klassifikationsbasierte Regression, um die Elektronentemperatur in der Plasmasphäre der Erde basierend auf Satellitendaten und geomagnetischen Indizes vorherzusagen und erreicht eine Genauigkeit von 69,67% innerhalb von 10% der Bodenwahrheit. Der diskretisierte Klassifikationsansatz verbessert die Vorhersagegenauigkeit um 6,46%, während er Unsicherheitsschätzungen liefert.

es.title: CLARE: Regresión basada en clasificación para la predicción de temperatura de electrones
es.summary: CLARE utiliza regresión basada en clasificación para predecir la temperatura de electrones en la plasmasfera terrestre a partir de datos satelitales e índices geomagnéticos, logrando una precisión del 69,67% dentro del 10% de la verdad del terreno. El enfoque de clasificación discretizado mejora la precisión de predicción en un 6,46% mientras proporciona estimaciones de incertidumbre.

title: The Perfection Paradox: From Architect to Curator in AI-Assisted API Design
summary: A study of AI-assisted API design found that AI-generated specifications outperformed human designs across 10 of 11 usability dimensions while reducing authoring time by 87%, yet experts often misidentified them as human work and described them as unsettlingly 'perfect.' The research introduces the 'Perfection Paradox,' suggesting that humans may perceive hyper-consistency as lacking pragmatic judgment, prompting a shift in designer roles from specification drafters to curators of AI patterns.
content:
arXiv:2603.12475v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Enterprise API design is often bottlenecked by the tension between rapid feature delivery and the rigorous maintenance of usability standards. We present an industrial case study evaluating an AI-assisted design workflow trained on API Improvement Proposals (AIPs). Through a controlled study with 16 industry experts, we compared AI-generated API specifications against human-authored ones. While quantitative results indicated AI superiority in 10 of 11 usability dimensions and an 87% reduction in authoring time, qualitative analysis revealed a paradox: experts frequently misidentified AI work as human (19% accuracy) yet described the designs as unsettlingly "perfect." We characterize this as a "Perfection Paradox" -- where hyper-consistency signals a lack of pragmatic human judgment. We discuss the implications of this perfection paradox, proposing a shift in the human designer's role from the "drafter" of specifications to the "curator" of AI-generated patterns.

zh.title: 完美悖论：AI辅助API设计中从架构师到策展人的转变
zh.summary: 一项AI辅助API设计的研究表明，AI生成的规范在11个可用性维度中的10个都超越了人工设计，同时将创作时间减少了87%。然而，专家经常误认为这些是人工设计，并将其描述为令人不安的"完美"。该研究引入了"完美悖论"的概念，表明人类可能将超级一致性视为缺乏实用判断，促使设计师的角色从规范制定者转变为AI模式的策展人。

fr.title: Le Paradoxe de la Perfection : Du Concepteur au Curateur dans la Conception d'API Assistée par l'IA
fr.summary: Une étude sur la conception d'API assistée par l'IA a révélé que les spécifications générées par l'IA ont surpassé les conceptions humaines dans 10 des 11 dimensions d'utilisabilité tout en réduisant le temps d'auteur de 87%. Cependant, les experts les identifiaient souvent à tort comme des travaux humains et les décrivaient comme étrangement « parfaits ». La recherche introduit le « Paradoxe de la Perfection », suggérant que les humains peuvent percevoir l'hyper-cohérence comme manquant de jugement pragmatique, incitant un changement dans les rôles des concepteurs.

de.title: Das Perfektionsparadoxon: Vom Architekt zum Kurator in der KI-gestützten API-Gestaltung
de.summary: Eine Studie zur KI-gestützten API-Gestaltung ergab, dass KI-generierte Spezifikationen in 10 von 11 Benutzerfreundlichkeitsdimensionen menschliche Entwürfe übertrafen und die Autorenzeit um 87% reduzierten. Experten identifizierten sie jedoch häufig fälschlicherweise als menschliche Arbeiten und beschrieben sie als verstörendperfekt. Die Forschung führt das « Perfektionsparadoxon » ein und deutet darauf hin, dass Menschen Überkonsistenz als mangelnde pragmatische Beurteilung wahrnehmen können, was zu einer Verschiebung der Designer-Rollen führt.

es.title: La Paradoja de la Perfección: Del Arquitecto al Curador en el Diseño de API Asistido por IA
es.summary: Un estudio del diseño de API asistido por IA encontró que las especificaciones generadas por IA superaron los diseños humanos en 10 de 11 dimensiones de usabilidad mientras reducía el tiempo de autoría en un 87%. Sin embargo, los expertos a menudo las identificaban erróneamente como trabajos humanos y las describían como desagradablemente « perfectas ». La investigación introduce la « Paradoja de la Perfección », sugiriendo que los humanos pueden percibir la hiperconsistencia como carente de juicio pragmático, impulsando un cambio en los roles de los diseñadores.

title: One-Step Flow Policy: Self-Distillation for Fast Visuomotor Policies
summary: Researchers propose One-Step Flow Policy (OFP), a self-distillation framework that generates robot actions in a single step rather than through iterative sampling, achieving 100x faster inference while surpassing 100-step diffusion policies. OFP unifies self-consistency loss and self-guided regularization to maintain high-fidelity action generation without pre-trained teachers.
content:
arXiv:2603.12480v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Generative flow and diffusion models provide the continuous, multimodal action distributions needed for high-precision robotic policies. However, their reliance on iterative sampling introduces severe inference latency, degrading control frequency and harming performance in time-sensitive manipulation. To address this problem, we propose the One-Step Flow Policy (OFP), a from-scratch self-distillation framework for high-fidelity, single-step action generation without a pre-trained teacher. OFP unifies a self-consistency loss to enforce coherent transport across time intervals, and a self-guided regularization to sharpen predictions toward high-density expert modes. In addition, a warm-start mechanism leverages temporal action correlations to minimize the generative transport distance. Evaluations across 56 diverse simulated manipulation tasks demonstrate that a one-step OFP achieves state-of-the-art results, outperforming 100-step diffusion and flow policies while accelerating action generation by over $100\times$. We further integrate OFP into the $\pi_{0.5}$ model on RoboTwin 2.0, where one-step OFP surpasses the original 10-step policy. These results establish OFP as a practical, scalable solution for highly accurate and low-latency robot control.

zh.title: 一步流策略：快速视觉运动策略的自蒸馏
zh.summary: 研究人员提出了一步流策略（OFP），一种自蒸馏框架，在单个步骤中生成机器人动作，而不是通过迭代采样，实现了100倍更快的推理，同时超越了100步扩散策略。OFP统一了自一致性损失和自引导正则化，以保持高保真度的动作生成，无需预训练教师。

fr.title: Politique de Flux Uniétape : Auto-Distillation pour les Politiques Visuomotrices Rapides
fr.summary: Les chercheurs proposent la Politique de Flux Uniétape (OFP), un cadre d'auto-distillation qui génère des actions de robot en une seule étape plutôt que par échantillonnage itératif, atteignant une inférence 100 fois plus rapide tout en surpassant les politiques de diffusion à 100 étapes. OFP unifie la perte d'auto-cohérence et la régularisation auto-guidée pour maintenir la génération d'actions haute fidélité sans enseignants pré-formés.

de.title: One-Step-Flow-Richtlinie: Selbst-Destillation für schnelle visuomotorische Richtlinien
de.summary: Forscher schlagen die One-Step-Flow-Richtlinie (OFP) vor, ein Selbst-Destillationsrahmen, der Roboterhandlungen in einem einzigen Schritt statt durch iteratives Sampling generiert und 100-mal schnellere Inferenzen erreicht und dabei 100-Schritt-Diffusionsrichtlinien übertrifft. OFP vereinheitlicht Selbst-Konsistenz-Verlust und selbstgelenkte Regularisierung, um hochwertige Aktionsgenerierung ohne vortrainierte Lehrer zu erhalten.

es.title: Política de Flujo de Un Paso: Auto-Destilación para Políticas Visuomotoras Rápidas
es.summary: Los investigadores proponen la Política de Flujo de Un Paso (OFP), un marco de auto-destilación que genera acciones de robot en un solo paso en lugar de mediante muestreo iterativo, logrando una inferencia 100 veces más rápida mientras supera las políticas de difusión de 100 pasos. OFP unifica la pérdida de auto-consistencia y la regularización auto-guiada para mantener la generación de acciones de alta fidelidad sin maestros previamente entrenados.

title: TRACE: Temporal Rule-Anchored Chain-of-Evidence on Knowledge Graphs for Interpretable Stock Movement Prediction
summary: TRACE combines knowledge graphs with news grounding and LLM reasoning to predict stock movements with interpretable evidence chains, achieving 55.1% accuracy and 60.8% F1 on S&P 500 data. The method uses rule-guided exploration to focus on economically meaningful patterns rather than arbitrary walks, delivering both predictive lift and human-readable decision paths.
content:
arXiv:2603.12500v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present a Temporal Rule-Anchored Chain-of-Evidence (TRACE) on knowledge graphs for interpretable stock movement prediction that unifies symbolic relational priors, dynamic graph exploration, and LLM-guided decision making in a single end-to-end pipeline. The approach performs rule-guided multi-hop exploration restricted to admissible relation sequences, grounds candidate reasoning chains in contemporaneous news, and aggregates fully grounded evidence into auditable \texttt{UP}/\texttt{DOWN} verdicts with human-readable paths connecting text and structure. On an S\&P~500 benchmark, the method achieves 55.1\% accuracy, 55.7\% precision, 71.5\% recall, and 60.8\% F1, surpassing strong baselines and improving recall and F1 over the best graph baseline under identical evaluation. The gains stem from (i) rule-guided exploration that focuses search on economically meaningful motifs rather than arbitrary walks, and (ii) text-grounded consolidation that selectively aggregates high-confidence, fully grounded hypotheses instead of uniformly pooling weak signals. Together, these choices yield higher sensitivity without sacrificing selectivity, delivering predictive lift with faithful, auditably interpretable explanations.

zh.title: TRACE：知识图谱上的时间规则锚定证据链用于可解释的股票运动预测
zh.summary: TRACE将知识图谱与新闻基础和LLM推理相结合，以可解释的证据链预测股票运动，在标普500指数数据上达到55.1%的准确率和60.8%的F1分数。该方法使用规则引导的探索来关注经济上有意义的模式，而不是任意游走，同时提供了预测提升和人类可读的决策路径。

fr.title: TRACE : Chaîne d'Évidence Ancrée sur Règles Temporelles sur des Graphes de Connaissance pour la Prédiction Interprétable des Mouvements Boursiers
fr.summary: TRACE combine des graphes de connaissance avec l'ancrage de nouvelles et le raisonnement LLM pour prédire les mouvements boursiers avec des chaînes d'évidence interprétables, réalisant une précision de 55,1% et un F1 de 60,8% sur les données du S&P 500. La méthode utilise l'exploration guidée par règles pour se concentrer sur les modèles économiquement significatifs plutôt que sur les promenades arbitraires, offrant à la fois une amélioration prédictive et des chemins de décision lisibles par l'homme.

de.title: TRACE: Zeitliche Regel-verankerte Beweiskette auf Wissensgraphen für interpretierbare Aktienbewegungsprognose
de.summary: TRACE kombiniert Wissensgraphen mit Nachrichtankerung und LLM-Reasoning, um Aktienbewegungen mit interpretierbaren Beweisketten vorherzusagen, erreicht eine Genauigkeit von 55,1% und einen F1 von 60,8% bei S&P-500-Daten. Die Methode verwendet regelgelenkte Erkundung, um sich auf wirtschaftlich bedeutungsvolle Muster statt beliebiger Spaziergänge zu konzentrieren und bietet sowohl prädiktive Verbesserung als auch menschenlesbare Entscheidungspfade.

es.title: TRACE: Cadena de Evidencia Anclada en Reglas Temporales en Gráficos de Conocimiento para Predicción Interpretable del Movimiento Bursátil
es.summary: TRACE combina gráficos de conocimiento con anclaje de noticias y razonamiento LLM para predecir movimientos bursátiles con cadenas de evidencia interpretables, logrando una precisión del 55,1% y un F1 del 60,8% en datos del S&P 500. El método utiliza exploración guiada por reglas para enfocarse en patrones económicamente significativos en lugar de paseos arbitrarios, entregando tanto un impulso predictivo como caminos de decisión legibles por humanos.

title: Na\"ive PAINE: Lightweight Text-to-Image Generation Improvement with Prompt Evaluation
summary: Naïve PAINE improves text-to-image diffusion models by predicting which initial noise seeds will produce higher-quality results and selectively using those seeds for generation. This lightweight approach integrates seamlessly into existing pipelines and outperforms existing methods on prompt corpus benchmarks without significant computational overhead.
content:
arXiv:2603.12506v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Text-to-Image (T2I) generation is primarily driven by Diffusion Models (DM) which rely on random Gaussian noise. Thus, like playing the slots at a casino, a DM will produce different results given the same user-defined inputs. This imposes a gambler's burden: To perform multiple generation cycles to obtain a satisfactory result.
